candidates.name: Maura Healey
candidates.notes: First woman and first openly lesbian governor of Massachusetts (elected 2022). Former AG who enforced assault weapons ban aggressively. Declared MA a sanctuary state for abortion and gender-affirming care. Opposes school choice. Civil rights attorney background. Strong gun control advocate who used AG powers to reinterpret assault weapons law.

profile.education: Harvard University (BA); Northeastern University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and former professional basketball player in Europe; Massachusetts Attorney General (2015-2023); became first elected female governor of Massachusetts in 2023.

candidates.name: Elizabeth Warren
candidates.notes: Senator since 2013. 2020 presidential candidate. Heritage Action 0% current, 11% lifetime. NRA F rated. Former Sunday school teacher. Supports Medicare for All, Green New Deal, assault weapons ban, red flag laws. Pro-choice. Supports pathway to citizenship. Opposes school vouchers. Created Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.

profile.education: University of Houston (BS Speech Pathology), Rutgers University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Harvard Law School professor and consumer protection advocate. Created Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. 2020 presidential candidate. Leading voice on economic inequality. In Senate since 2013.

district_offices.address: 15 New Sudbury Street, 2400 JFK Federal Building

candidates.name: Edward Markey
candidates.notes: Senator since 2013 (served in House 1976-2013). Heritage Action 0% current, 11% lifetime. NRA F rated. Co-author of Green New Deal with AOC. Irish Catholic raised in Malden, graduate of four Catholic institutions (Boston College). Supports assault weapons ban. Pro-choice despite Catholic upbringing. Longest-serving member from MA in history.

profile.education: Boston College (BA International Relations), Boston College Law School (JD)
profile.background: Former U.S. House member for 37 years, one of the longest-serving in history. Co-author of Green New Deal. Served in Army Reserve. Former Massachusetts state representative.

district_offices.address: 15 New Sudbury St, Suite 975, JFK Federal Building

candidates.name: Karen Spilka
candidates.notes: President of the Massachusetts Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Massachusetts_Senate.
